mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered months, or the signal's out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains below 0.1 over a 3-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive mean 60-day return when both series grow quarter-over-quarter, or the interaction term's coefficient loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a quarterly regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity utilization approaching maximums, which raises inflation expectations and prompts equity market pullbacks over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns remain positive during periods of industrial production acceleration within late-cycle regimes, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when regime filters are applied.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate surpassing 4.5 percent imposes financing constraints on highly leveraged corporate sectors, triggering immediate sell-offs that yield negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal generates positive 5-day returns in over 60% of occurrences, or the conditional probability of a negative return does not exceed 50% when the rate crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and a stable or falling unemployment rate indicates labor market tightening that compresses corporate profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when PAYEMS grows by more than 150k monthly while UNRATE declines, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising household confidence and construction sector activity, which directly boosts related equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the housing starts signal remains below 0.01 across all 20-day forward return windows, or positive returns do not materialize in more than 50% of months with rising housing starts.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 100 baseline index during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply-side weakness that undermines near-term earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day returns in over 55% of triggered instances within post-shock recovery regimes, or the conditional return distribution shows no statistically significant negative skew.
